title: COLLATE operator on lhs of BETWEEN expression is ignored.
description:
dan added on 2013-08-21 10:56:31:
According to documentation here: http://www.sqlite.org/datatype3.html
"The expression "x BETWEEN y and z" is logically equivalent to two comparisons "x >= y AND x <= z" and works with respect to collating functions as if it were two separate comparisons."
However:
SQLite version 3.7.17 2013-05-17 12:52:58
Enter ".help" for instructions
Enter SQL statements terminated with a ";"
sqlite> CREATE TABLE t1(x);
sqlite> INSERT INTO t1 VALUES('b');
sqlite> INSERT INTO t1 VALUES('B');
sqlite> SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE x COLLATE nocase BETWEEN 'a' AND 'c';
b
sqlite> SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE x COLLATE nocase >= 'a' AND x COLLATE nocase <= 'c';
b
B
dan added on 2013-08-21 10:57:52:
Introduced here: [8542e6180d4321d4]
First appeared in version 3.7.15.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(x VARCHAR(255), y INTEGER);
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE UPPER(x) BETWEEN UPPER('a') AND UPPER('c');
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE UPPER(x) >= UPPER('a') AND UPPER(x) <= UPPER('c') AND y IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: COLLATE operator ignored in BETWEEN expression
